overview: A front-end only college discovery platform, 'College Finder', built with Next.js 13, React 18, HTML, and CSSv4. Key features include college listings with search and filters, detailed college pages (sections, placement stats, reviews), side-by-side comparison (up to three colleges), saving colleges and comparisons, and a full client-side login/signup flow with local storage persistence. The platform requires no backend, API keys, or configuration, designed for simple `npm install` and `npm render`.
discussion_points: **Project Architecture**: Strict architectural separation was maintained, with domain components (e.g., college, detail) distinct from UI components (e.g., button, card). UI components are pure, prop-driven primitives using Tailwind, ensuring reusability and allowing isolated visual design changes without affecting feature files.

**State Management (Zustand vs. React Context)**: Zustand was chosen over React Context for `auth`, `compare`, and `save` stores due to its performance benefits. Zustand allows components to subscribe to specific state slices, triggering re-renders only when those specific values change, unlike React Context's broader re-renders for all subscribers on any change.

**Persistent State**: Zustand's persistent middleware automatically serializes store state (e.g., saved colleges) to local storage for persistence across refreshes. The trade-off of synchronous local storage potentially blocking the main thread during hydration was noted, suggesting IndexedDB for large production datasets.

**Hooks and Page Logic**: All listing page logic (filtering, sorting, pagination, 'view more') is encapsulated in a single custom hook (`useColleges`), adhering to the 'pages should be thin' principle where page components act as pure coordinators.

**Search Debouncing and Memoization**: Implemented 250ms search input debouncing using a custom `useDebounce` hook to prevent excessive filter re-computations. Filter results are memoized using `useMemo` (depending only on filters, search, and sort key) to optimize performance and avoid unnecessary re-computation.

**Filtering Edge Case**: An edge case where changing filters could lead to an empty grid if the user was on a page beyond the new filter results was addressed by automatically resetting the search to page 1.

**Compare Feature Implementation**: The compare feature uses a `toComparableCollegeMapper` to transform partial listing data into detailed comparison data. Comparison metrics are defined as extensible data objects (with `label`, `getValue`, and optional `highlightAction`), allowing easy addition of new metrics. A hardcoded comparison limit in the compare bar was centralized for maintainability.

**CSS and Design System**: The design system uses global CSS custom properties (e.g., `--sapphire`) referenced via Tailwind's arbitrary values (e.g., `bg-[--sapphire]`). A development bug where `bg-sapphire` generated invalid CSS was identified and fixed by a script. Buttons were refactored to use concrete Tailwind classes to reduce indirection.

**Accessibility (A11y) Features**: Implemented accessibility features including a focus trap for modals, `aria-pressed` and `aria-label` for Save/Compare buttons to convey their toggle state and provide full context, and `aria-current` on active section navigation for detail pages, driven by an Intersection Observer.
action_items: Introduce a server state management library (e.g., React Query, SWR) for a real product, while maintaining the `useColleges` hook interface.
Replace local storage persistence with HTTP-only cookies and server-side validation for all state in a production environment to enhance security.
Develop a comprehensive test suite, including Cypress E2E and Vitest unit tests, prioritizing this over feature development in future projects, leveraging the existing testable architecture.
decisions: Project components are strictly separated into UI (pure primitives) and domain components (compose primitives, read from stores).
Zustand was chosen over React Context for `auth`, `compare`, and `save` stores due to its performance benefits with selective state subscriptions.
Zustand's persistent middleware was implemented for automatic local storage serialization and re-hydration of store data.
All shared project interfaces were consolidated into a single `college.ts` file for clean separation.
All listing page logic (filtering, sorting, pagination) was encapsulated within a single custom hook, making page components thin coordinators.
Search input was debounced at 250ms using a custom `useDebounce` hook for performance optimization.
Filter results were memoized using `useMemo` to prevent unnecessary re-computations.
Logic was implemented to reset the search to page 1 when filters are changed to prevent empty result grids.
The compare feature was designed to map additional data only when needed for comparison and to represent comparison metrics as extensible data objects.
CSS custom property usage with Tailwind's arbitrary values (e.g., `bg-[--sapphire]`) was corrected after identifying invalid CSS generation from a simplified syntax.
CSS files for buttons were replaced with concrete Tailwind classes to reduce indirection for critical interactive elements.
A focus trap mechanism for modals was implemented to improve keyboard navigation and screen reader experience.
`aria-pressed` and `aria-label` were used on save and compare buttons to convey their toggle state and provide full context to assistive technologies.
`aria-current` was employed on active section navigation buttons, driven by an Intersection Observer, to indicate the currently viewed section.
